meta.title: From Calling to Breakthrough: A Leader's Journey Through the DeVos Urban Leadership Initiative
meta.subtitle: training-workshop

overview:
This training workshop is built on a single, animating conviction drawn directly from the DeVos Urban Leadership Initiative (DVULI): healthy leaders produce healthy organizations, and healthy organizations build healthy communities. Over sixteen sessions, participants move through an intentional arc — from the inside out — beginning with ruthlessly honest self-discovery, progressing through five foundational Core Values, and culminating in a written, presented, and accountability-backed Breakthrough Plan. Every concept introduced in the early sessions reappears in later ones at greater depth and complexity, so that by Session 16 participants are not merely recalling what they learned in Session 1 — they are living it differently.

The pedagogical philosophy of this workshop is grounded in the DVULI conviction that 'the most important outcome of your time in the program will be that you will learn to think differently — about yourself, your ministry, and your personal life.' This is not a content-delivery workshop; it is a transformation architecture. Sessions 1–4 excavate the participant's divine design through the Biblical DISC, the Leadership Strategy Assessment, the three-fold purpose framework, and the Leadership Tree synthesis. Sessions 5–8 introduce the five Core Values — Accountability, Balance, Interdependence, Empowerment, and Leverage — as non-negotiable pillars of sustainable ministry. Sessions 9–11 zoom outward to community, equipping participants with asset-based and youth development frameworks. Sessions 12–16 provide the strategic and systems-level tools — Scenario Planning, Systems Thinking, the Outward Mindset, Resource Networking, and Collaborative Leadership — that enable participants to convert their personal insights into a Breakthrough Plan that is strategic, significant, and sustainable.

What makes this workshop distinctive is its relentless integration. The Personal Assessment Model (Calling + Character + Commitment to Change) introduced in Session 1 threads through every subsequent session. The reflective journaling loop, activated in Session 4, becomes the primary learning artifact that participants bring to their Breakthrough presentations in Session 16. The five Core Values are not taught in isolation — they appear woven into community development exercises, systems thinking scenarios, and resource networking case studies. Facilitators should understand that their role is less instructor than companion on a learning journey: creating conditions for honest introspection, courageous community, and the kind of holy discomfort that produces genuine breakthrough.
